question_text: A student has two blocks made of different materials. Block A has a mass of 500 g and a volume of 250 cm³. Block B has a mass of 300 g and a volume of 100 cm³. Calculate the density of each block and determine which material would float in water (density of water = 1 g/cm³). Show your working.
correct_answer: Block A: density = 500 ÷ 250 = 2 g/cm³. Block B: density = 300 ÷ 100 = 3 g/cm³. Block A would float because its density (2 g/cm³) is greater than water (1 g/cm³), so it would sink. Neither block would float as both have densities greater than water.
explanation: To find density, divide mass by volume. Block A has a density of 2 g/cm³ and Block B has 3 g/cm³. Both are denser than water (1 g/cm³), so both would sink, not float. Objects less dense than water float.
